query: We're reviewing the Master Services Agreement before signing. Our GC wants precise answers to three things: (1) what is the vendor's total liability cap, (2) does that cap apply to a data breach caused by the vendor's negligence, and (3) are there any carve-outs where no cap applies at all? Please answer precisely and point to the relevant clauses.
answer:
Here is what the Master Services Agreement provides, clause by clause:

1. Total liability cap. Section 9.1 caps each party's aggregate liability at the total fees paid or payable by Customer during the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim. It is a rolling trailing-twelve-months-of-fees figure, not a fixed dollar amount, and it is expressly subject to Sections 9.3 and 9.4.

2. Data breach caused by vendor negligence. This is handled separately. Section 9.3 sets an enhanced 'super-cap' for data-protection breaches: the vendor's liability for a breach of its Data Protection Addendum obligations, including a Security Incident, is capped at three (3) times the trailing-twelve-months fees. So a negligent data breach is not unlimited, but it is capped higher than the general 9.1 cap.

3. Carve-outs where no cap applies. Section 9.4 lists the exclusions: (a) each party's indemnification obligations for third-party IP claims under Section 10, (b) a breach of the confidentiality section arising from willful misconduct, (c) either party's gross negligence or willful misconduct, and (d) any liability that cannot be limited under applicable law. Note that ordinary (non-willful) negligence is not carved out, so a merely negligent data breach is governed by the 3x super-cap in 9.3 rather than being uncapped.

Bottom line for your GC: the general cap is twelve months' fees (9.1); a negligent data breach is capped at 3x annual fees (9.3); and the only fully uncapped exposures are IP indemnity, willful confidentiality breaches, gross negligence/willful misconduct, and anything applicable law will not allow you to cap (9.4).
retrieved_docs: 9.1 Limitation of Liability. Except as set forth in Sections 9.3 and 9.4, each party's total aggregate liability arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid or payable by Customer to Vendor during the twelve (12) months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

9.3 Enhanced Cap for Data Protection. Notwithstanding Section 9.1, Vendor's aggregate liability for breach of its obligations under the Data Protection Addendum, including any Security Incident, shall not exceed three (3) times the fees paid or payable during the twelve (12) months preceding the incident.

9.4 Exclusions from Limitation. The limitations in Sections 9.1 and 9.3 shall not apply to: (a) either party's indemnification obligations under Section 10 (Third-Party IP Claims); (b) a party's breach of Section 7 (Confidentiality) arising from willful misconduct; (c) either party's gross negligence or willful misconduct; or (d) any liability that cannot be excluded or limited under applicable law.

citations.claim: total fees paid or payable by Customer during the twelve (12) months preceding the event
capped at three (3) times the trailing-twelve-months fees
ordinary (non-willful) negligence is not carved out
